title: Breathe Out the Storm
lesson: When big angry feelings come, taking slow deep breaths helps you feel calm again.
prompts: In a bright playroom in the afternoon, warm light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits facing the camera with a happy, focused expression and the face fully visible, stacking colourful building blocks into a tall tower. Medium shot at eye level, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright playroom in the afternoon, warm light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits facing the camera with an angry, frustrated expression and the face fully visible, fists clenched, as the colourful block tower tumbles down around them. Scattered blocks on the floor. Medium shot at eye level, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright playroom in the afternoon, warm light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the far left in the mid-ground, arms crossed tightly and shoulders tense, facing the camera with an upset expression and the face fully visible. In the right foreground, {GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} kneels close, one hand on their own belly, calmly showing how to take a deep breath. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright playroom in the afternoon, warm light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the far left with eyes gently closed and one hand on their chest taking a slow deep breath, head turned toward the camera so the face stays clearly visible, the angry frown softening. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} breathes along encouragingly. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright playroom in the afternoon, warm light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits with both hands resting loosely in their lap, facing the camera with a calm, relieved little smile and the face fully visible, shoulders relaxed, the storm of feelings passed. Medium shot at eye level, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright playroom in the afternoon, warm light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the far left calmly rebuilding the block tower, facing the camera with a peaceful, happy smile and the face fully visible. On the right, {GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} helps place a block, smiling proudly. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
gists: In a bright afternoon playroom, the child sits alone happily stacking colourful blocks into a tall tower, focused.
In the playroom, the child sits alone, angry and frustrated with fists clenched, as the block tower tumbles down around them.
In the playroom, the child (far left) sits with arms crossed tightly, upset, while the father at right kneels close with a hand on his own belly, showing how to take a deep breath.
In the playroom, the child (far left) sits with eyes gently closed and a hand on their chest taking a slow deep breath, the angry frown softening, while the father at right breathes along.
In the playroom, the child sits alone with hands resting loosely in their lap, a calm relieved little smile and relaxed shoulders — the angry storm has passed.
In the playroom, the child (far left) calmly rebuilds the block tower with a peaceful smile while the father at right helps place a block, proud — deep breaths chased the storm away.
texts: In the playroom you stacked your blocks into a tall, wobbly tower. Higher and higher!
Then it all tumbled down. You felt SO angry, your fists clenched up tight.
Dad knelt down beside you. "Let's take a deep breath," he said, his hand on his belly.
You closed your eyes and breathed in slow... and out. The angry storm began to fade away.
Soon you felt calm again, your shoulders soft and a little smile on your face.
Feeling better, you built your tower again — this time with Dad. Deep breaths chase the storm away.
